L1.description: An authorization bypass vulnerability exists in the remote procedure invocation path where user-controlled parameters are forwarded to execution hosts without sanitizing internal control fields. An authenticated client can inject approval-related boolean flags and decision values into the request payload, causing the receiving host to treat the command as pre-approved and skip the interactive approval workflow. This allows arbitrary command execution on connected hosts without proper authorization.

ground_truth.reason: The gateway forwarded user-controlled params to node hosts without stripping internal approval fields (approved, approvalDecision), allowing an authenticated client to bypass the exec approval workflow and execute arbitrary commands on connected node hosts.
